chunk_summary: Disease-specific enteral formulas in India focus on tailored macronutrient profiles for conditions like CKD, cholestasis, and chronic lung disease, with modular substitutions and safety protocols for blenderized tube feeding (BTF).
learning_objectives: Understand the role of disease-specific enteral formulas in managing chronic conditions like CKD, cholestasis, and chronic lung disease.
Learn modular substitution strategies for macronutrients in standard formulas when disease-specific products are unavailable.
Recognize safety protocols for blenderized tube feeding (BTF) in pediatric patients, including food safety and hang time considerations.
key_facts: In chronic kidney disease (CKD), controlled potassium and phosphate exposure, strict fluid budgeting, and protein needs are critical.
For cholestasis, focus on fat type tolerance and fat-soluble vitamin status; avoid dilution strategies that reduce micronutrient density.
In chronic lung disease with CO2 retention, avoid overfeeding, especially excess carbohydrates, and align calories to measured or estimated REE.
Standard polymeric formulas with modular additions are safer and cheaper alternatives to frequent formula switching.
Blenderized tube feeding (BTF) requires standardized recipes, strict food safety, and short hang times to prevent complications like tube blockage.
reference_quote: In chronic kidney disease, energy-dense standard formulas with carefully prescribed modular additions may be safer and cheaper than frequent formula switching. (Source: Indian Pediatrics Review, April 2020)
